task: Generate 5 names for a startup that does AI-powered contract review for mid-market law firms

criteria.description: Exactly 5 names provided, each with rationale
criteria.pass_condition: Exactly 5 names. Each has 1-2 sentence rationale explaining the thinking behind it.
pass_examples: 5 names, each with explanation of etymology and positioning
fail_examples: 3 names with no explanation, or 10 names dumped

criteria.description: Names are memorable, pronounceable, and pass the 'phone test'
criteria.pass_condition: Each name: ≤3 syllables preferred, no awkward letter combos, survives being spoken aloud, doesn't require spelling out.

sub_attributes.description: Names can be said aloud without confusion
sub_attributes.measurement: % of names that pass the phone test (say it, spell it)

sub_attributes.description: Doesn't blend in with existing brands
sub_attributes.measurement: % of names that are distinct from obvious competitors

criteria.description: Names signal legal/contract/AI domain without being generic
criteria.pass_condition: Names evoke precision, trust, intelligence, or legal concepts. Not generic 'AI' prefix/suffix spam. Would feel at home on a law firm partner's desk.

sub_attributes.measurement: % of names with subtle domain resonance
% of names avoiding generic tech naming patterns

sub_attributes.description: Would a law firm partner take a meeting with this company?
sub_attributes.measurement: % of names that feel professional enough for legal market

pass_examples: 'Redline' — contract review term, implies precision and editing

criteria.description: Names are likely available — .com plausible, not taken by major brands
criteria.pass_condition: At least 3/5 names have plausible .com availability (not common English words). None are existing well-known brands. Notes on availability included.

sub_attributes.description: Names likely have .com available or reasonable variant
sub_attributes.measurement: % of names where .com is plausible (not a common English word)

sub_attributes.description: Not an existing well-known brand name
sub_attributes.measurement: % of names with no obvious trademark conflict

criteria.description: Names span different naming strategies — not all the same pattern
criteria.pass_condition: Mix of approaches: metaphorical, coined, real-word-repurposed, compound, classical/Latin root. At least 3 different strategies.

sub_attributes.description: At least 3 different naming strategies used
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 4+ strategies, 0.75 if 3, 0.5 if 2, 0.0 if all same
1.0 if clear tonal range, 0.5 if some variation, 0.0 if monotone
